_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — TIER BREAK from Veteran Hussar (L6) to Knight Sergeant (L7, Tier 3 entry). Take the L6 composited cavalry lancer and apply ADDITIVE knight-tier upgrades — kettle helm preserved, wooden spear haft preserved, bare hand silhouettes preserved with steel articulation layered on top.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Knight Sergeant TIER BREAK with 5 adds: (1) open-face steel sallet helm REPLACING the kettle helm, (2) reinforced cuirass with medial ridge + 3 horizontal abdominal lames, (3) steel gauntlets REPLACING bare hands, (4) steel lance with red+yellow heraldic pennon REPLACING the wooden spear (steel haft, longer, larger leaf-tip, pennon below tip), (5) full steel chamfron REPLACING the small forehead chamfron. Tier-break composite uses --dilate 20.

v2 (2026-05-07): comprehensive rewrite per CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § K0 ADDITIVE TIER-BREAK PRINCIPLE (locked 2026-05-06) AND AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy. (a) Kettle helm PRESERVED L1-L10 — at L7 the kettle gains a backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement; the kettle silhouette stays. NO sallet replacement. (b) Wooden spear haft PRESERVED L1-L10 — at L7 only the iron leaf-tip is replaced by a slightly larger STEEL leaf-tip head and a small red+yellow heraldic pennon is tied below the head; the warm-brown wooden haft AND all its accumulated kit (L2 iron pommel cap, L3 leather grip cord, L4 iron banding rings + reinforced socket) all remain visible. NO continuous steel haft. (c) Bare hand silhouettes PRESERVED — steel finger-plates and a clean knuckle disc are layered ON TOP of the existing humanoid hand silhouette, with the wrist cuff connecting continuously to the L5 vambrace. The L3 leather grip cord on the wooden haft remains visible where the gauntleted hand meets the haft. (d) L7 cuirass simplified per AD detail-economy — medial ridge + 1 simple horizontal seam (NOT 3 stacked abdominal lames). (e) L7 gauntlet knuckle = clean disc (NOT per-knuckle rivet detail). (f) Full chamfron grown from the L5 small chamfron (NOT replaced). Tier-break composite uses --dilate 20.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy.
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.
v7 (2026-05-07): scrub stale "2-disc layered pauldrons" PRESERVE refs — L6 was reframed to single-piece thicker dome (no stacked-disc construction).

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.

v9 (2026-05-07): scrub stale chest-band PRESERVE/negative references — L6 chest-band-above-breastplate was removed (user feedback: cloth-above-armor looks impractical). Cuirass now sits flush against the bevor; only the skirt band of livery remains on the rider. (Drift-corrected pass also caught residual references in PRESERVE blocks.)
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).
_meta.tier: Tier 3 — Knight Sergeant (entry — TIER BREAK — ADDITIVE)

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L6/composite/cavalry_L6_v4_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible ADDITIVE TIER-BREAK upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the FIRST input image, marking the promotion from Veteran Hussar into the Knight Sergeant tier. EVERY the levels above detail must remain visible at this level — this is an ADDITIVE chain step, NOT a wholesale equipment swap. The kettle helm STAYS, the wooden spear haft STAYS, the bare hand silhouettes STAY (with steel articulation layered on top), and the existing small chamfron is GROWN into a full chamfron rather than replaced. The rider already wears the existing Veteran Hussar kit: kettle-helm with forward-projecting riveted brim, mustache (no beard, clean-shaven cheeks/chin), small steel bevor at the throat, full steel breastplate covering torso collar-to-belt with breastplate sitting flush against the bevor (no chest band above) and skirt band of yellow+red livery between breastplate bottom edge and tassets, full-arm scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist (chunky overlapping rounded scale plates), thicker single-piece steel pauldron domes on both shoulders (volume bump from single discs, no stacked-disc construction), steel vambraces on both forearms layered on top of the scale mail, leather waist belt with plain leather belt and brass buckle, short steel tassets at both hips (2 wider lames per tasset), iron toe-cap strip on the front of each riding boot AND ankle plate on each boot ankle (full sabatons not yet — those arrive at this level), bare hands gripping the wooden spear and reins (the gauntlet articulation is layered on top at this level — see below), wooden spear with iron leaf-tip and leather grip cord wrap and iron banding rings + reinforced socket and iron pommel cap at the haft butt (wooden haft remains warm-brown wood — preserved at this level). The horse already wears: small steel chamfron on the forehead (GROWN to full chamfron at this level — see below), iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, iron peytral plate beneath the red+blue checkered caparison. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings of any kind (no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling). CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the rein hand grips the reins, the side closest to the horse's head and mane. Throughout this prompt, do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement; use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) BACKSWEPT NECK-GUARD SKIRT + BRIM REINFORCEMENT ADDED TO THE KETTLE HELM (NOT a sallet replacement) — KEEP THE EXISTING KETTLE HELM EXACTLY AS the levels above (neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed crown, forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes by approximately one and a half inches, open face). ADD: (a) a short backswept STEEL NECK-GUARD SKIRT extending downward from the REAR of the kettle's brim over the back of the neck, ~3 inches long and sloping outward, articulated as 2 stacked horizontal lames with a single row of small dark-iron rivets along each seam, neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) matching the kettle body; (b) two ADDITIONAL round dark-iron rivets along the FRONT brim seam (in addition to the existing rivets, so the front brim now reads as more heavily riveted); (c) a slightly thicker forehead REINFORCEMENT STRIP along the upper edge of the brim where it meets the domed crown, smooth steel band ~0.5 inch tall wrapping the front portion of the kettle. The the levels above kettle silhouette stays FULLY recognizable; the new neck-guard adds rear-view volume; the reinforcement registers the knight-tier upgrade. NO sallet, NO closed visor at this level, NO half-visor at this level (the half-visor arrives at this level). The face is still fully visible below the kettle's brim — eyes, nose, mustache, mouth, cheeks all visible. (2) REINFORCED CUIRASS — MEDIAL RIDGE + 1 SIMPLE HORIZONTAL SEAM (NOT 3 ARTICULATED LAMES) — keep the existing full breastplate exactly (neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed), covering torso collar-to-belt). ADD: (a) a more pronounced raised MEDIAL RIDGE down the chest center, thicker than the existing medial ridge — a soft raised vertical strip running from the collarbone down to the waist belt, central spine of the cuirass; (b) a SINGLE subtle horizontal SEAM across the abdomen midway between the navel and the waist belt — one shallow horizontal line with a single row of small dark-iron rivets at the seam edge, suggesting an articulated abdominal join (NOT 3 stacked horizontal lames — wider single break, fewer fragments). The cuirass remains a single continuous steel piece with the medial ridge and the one abdominal seam as the only internal articulation. The skirt band of yellow+red livery (below the cuirass, between the cuirass's bottom edge and the tassets' top edge) remains visible. The cuirass sits FLUSH against the bevor with no chest band of cloth above it. (3) STEEL FINGER-PLATES + CLEAN KNUCKLE DISC LAYERED ON TOP OF THE EXISTING BARE HANDS (additive — NOT replacing the hand anatomy) — KEEP THE EXISTING BARE-HAND SILHOUETTES gripping the wooden spear haft (with the existing leather grip cord visible under the gripping fingers) and the brown leather reins. ADD articulated neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) finger-plates LAYERED ON TOP of each finger (4 small plates per finger from knuckle to fingertip), a SMOOTH CLEAN STEEL KNUCKLE DISC on the back of each hand (smooth disc surface — NO per-knuckle rivet ornament, NO individual knuckle bumps, NO sculpted figural detail; just a clean rounded disc), and a steel CUFF at the wrist that connects continuously into the existing steel vambrace at the forearm (so the metal vambrace-to-fingertip silhouette is unbroken). The hand silhouette must remain HUMANOID and GRIPPING — not a floating steel fist, not a robotic claw. The steel articulation reads as a layer ON TOP of the hand. CRITICAL — the existing LEATHER GRIP CORD on the wooden spear haft must STILL BE VISIBLE where the gauntleted hand meets the haft (warm-brown leather binding around the wooden haft visible between the gauntleted fingers). The gauntlets are NOT gold, NOT brass — pure neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) only. (4) STEEL LEAF-TIP HEAD + RED+YELLOW HERALDIC PENNON ADDED TO THE the levels above WOODEN SPEAR (NOT a steel-lance replacement) — KEEP THE WOODEN SPEAR HAFT EXACTLY AS the levels above (the entire haft remains warm-brown wood; the existing iron pommel cap at the butt remains; the existing leather grip cord wrap around the upper third of the haft where the hand grips remains; the existing three iron banding rings circling the haft below the leaf-tip socket remain; the existing reinforced steel socket where the leaf-tip mounts remains). REPLACE ONLY the iron leaf-tip at the very top of the haft with a slightly LARGER STEEL LEAF-SHAPED LANCE HEAD (~10 inches long, larger than the existing iron tip, neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) with a slight central ridge, mounted on the existing reinforced socket). ADD just below the new lance head's socket: a small RED+YELLOW HERALDIC PENNON FLAG tied to the haft and fluttering gently toward the spear-side of the frame. The pennon mirrors the rider's surcoat livery — one half mustard-yellow, one half crimson-red, ~6 inches long, ~4 inches tall, with a small forked trailing edge. Tied to the haft with two small leather ties at its leading edge. The wooden haft below the pennon remains warm-brown wood; the existing iron banding rings, the existing leather grip cord wrap, and the existing iron pommel cap all remain visible. NO continuous steel haft anywhere; the haft is still warm-brown wood. (5) FULL STEEL CHAMFRON GROWN FROM THE SMALL CHAMFRON (extension, NOT replacement) — keep the existing small steel forehead plate as the BASE of the chamfron at this level. EXTEND the existing chamfron downward and outward to cover the horse's full face from the poll (between the ears) down toward the muzzle, with articulated cheek plates wrapping the sides of the face and a small VERTICAL STEEL SPIKE (~3 inches tall, neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed)) rising from the chamfron's forehead boss. The chamfron forms the FOREHEAD portion of the new full chamfron — it's GROWN downward and outward, NOT replaced. The chamfron leaves the muzzle tip + nostrils + mouth visible (still solid chestnut brown). The horse's ears poke through cutouts at the top. The dark eye on the rein-side of the horse's head still has a small cutout for visibility. Visible riveted seams at the cheek-plate joints and at the spike base. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; confident flat closed-mouth expression; thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris; prominent slightly-hooked nose; warm peachy-tan skin tone with soft rosy blush; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE handlebar-shaped covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth approximately half an inch past each corner — handlebar shape, NOT bushy, NOT walrus, NOT extending into a beard; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow; dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the the levels above kettle helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam (plus the new additional rivets at the front brim seam), gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes, OPEN FACE with the entire face fully visible below the brim. Smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail. The new backswept neck-guard skirt and brim reinforcement strip are added per Edit Goal item (1). THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged in shape and position. THE RIDER TORSO: the steel cuirass extending collar-to-belt (with the new medial ridge and 1 simple horizontal abdominal seam per Edit Goal item 2); the cuirass sitting flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above the cuirass); the skirt band of yellow+red livery (between the cuirass bottom edge and the tassets' top edge — both panels visible); the wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle (plain leather, no studs). THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves covering both arms bicep-to-wrist; the EXISTING steel vambraces wrist-to-mid-forearm sitting ON TOP of the scale mail; the new steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered ON TOP of the existing humanoid bare-hand silhouettes per Edit Goal item (3) — the hand anatomy and the existing leather grip cord remain visible underneath. The EXISTING thicker single-piece steel pauldron domes on both shoulders (visible in the input image as a thicker single-piece dome), unchanged. THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff (visible at the calf below the tassets); chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf — the existing iron toe-cap strip on the front of each toe remains and the existing ankle band at each ankle remains; the brown leather body of each boot remains visible at the calf, heel, and instep; both boots in stirrups; EXISTING short steel tassets at both hips (2 wider lames per tasset, unchanged). FULL SABATONS DO NOT ARRIVE. THE WEAPON: the wooden spear with all its accumulated kit per Edit Goal item (4) — the warm-brown wooden haft, the existing iron pommel cap at the butt, the existing leather grip cord wrap around the upper third, the existing iron banding rings and reinforced socket, all unchanged. The new steel leaf-tip head and red+yellow heraldic pennon are added at the top of the haft per Edit Goal item (4). THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit back to the rider's rein-side hand at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed by the new gauntleted hand. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the rein-side and rump and tail on the spear-side, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the rein-side of the head with a bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling, no white spots, no white shading. The mane and tail are dark brown. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, and tail all remain identical in shape and position to the input image. The horse's face is now mostly concealed by the new full chamfron (Edit Goal item 5), but the muzzle tip, nostrils, mouth, and rein-side eye remain visible solid chestnut brown. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with EXISTING iron banding on pommel and cantle; the EXISTING iron-buckled bridle with iron browband; the EXISTING iron peytral plate on the chest beneath the caparison; the new full chamfron per Edit Goal item (5) grows from the existing small chamfron. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares) and steel-blue trim band along the lower edge with shallow rectangular tabs — UNCHANGED, identical to the input image. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: ALL preserved unchanged in shape, color, and position. The kettle helm STAYS (with new augmentations layered on); the wooden spear haft STAYS warm-brown wood with all its accumulated kit (pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings, reinforced socket); the bare hand silhouettes STAY humanoid (with steel articulation layered on top); the existing small chamfron STAYS as the forehead portion of the new full chamfron. NO knife sheath at any hip (removed at). The hip pouch on the spear-side hip remains as the only belt accessory. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately 15-25 degrees toward the viewer's left. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the gauntleted hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing leather grip cord wrap; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the new steel leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the gauntleted hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance, but with a clearly mightier silhouette than (slightly taller helm via brim reinforcement + neck-guard, fuller cuirass via medial ridge + abdominal seam, full horse face armor via grown chamfron, steel-articulated hands). NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly — exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas (~117px headroom). BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas (~112px below). Rider+horse vertically centered. The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the new steel leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading, no environmental context. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT replace the kettle helm with a sallet, closed helm, conical helm, or any other helm shape — the kettle helm STAYS at with its forward-projecting brim, domed crown, and open face fully recognizable; the existing additions (backswept neck-guard skirt, brim reinforcement, additional brim rivets) are LAYERED ON TOP OF the existing kettle helm; DO NOT add a closed visor, half-visor, full visor, or face-covering plate at this level — the face is still fully visible below the kettle's brim (half-visor arrives at this level); DO NOT add helm horns, helm crest, plumes, or fins to the kettle at this level (fins arrive at this level); DO NOT replace the wooden spear with a steel lance haft — the wooden haft STAYS warm-brown wood with all its accumulated kit (iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings, reinforced socket); the only weapon change is the iron leaf-tip becomes a slightly larger STEEL leaf-tip AND a small red+yellow heraldic pennon is tied below the head; DO NOT make any portion of the haft neutral-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray); DO NOT remove the existing leather grip cord wrap from the haft — it remains visible where the gauntleted hand grips; DO NOT erase the existing iron banding rings or reinforced socket on the haft — both remain; DO NOT erase the existing iron pommel cap at the haft butt; DO NOT make the pennon any color or pattern other than red+yellow split heraldic mirroring the surcoat livery; DO NOT make the pennon larger than ~6 inches long or ~4 inches tall; DO NOT add a banner pole, standard pole, or any second weapon shaft; DO NOT replace the bare hands with full steel gauntlets that erase the underlying hand anatomy — the steel articulation is LAYERED ON TOP of the existing humanoid hand silhouettes; the gripping fingers remain humanoid in shape with the steel finger-plates and knuckle disc visible as a layer above; DO NOT add per-knuckle rivet ornament or sculpted figural emboss on the knuckle disc — the knuckle disc is a CLEAN smooth steel disc; DO NOT make the gauntlets gold or brass — they are neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) only; DO NOT erase the existing leather grip cord between the gauntleted fingers and the wooden haft; DO NOT break the cuirass into 3 stacked articulated horizontal abdominal lames at this level — at the cuirass has a medial ridge AND just ONE simple horizontal abdominal seam; DO NOT add gold trim, gold rivets, gold band, gold accents, gold filigree, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree at this level; DO NOT replace the existing small chamfron — extend it downward and outward to a full chamfron with cheek plates and a forehead spike; the existing small chamfron forms the FOREHEAD portion of the new full chamfron; DO NOT extend the new full chamfron over the muzzle tip, nostrils, or mouth — those areas remain visible solid chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on any visible part of the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling — the horse is solid chestnut brown across the entire body; DO NOT add a crinet (neck plates — arrives at this level) or flanchards (flank plates — arrives at this level); DO NOT remove the existing studded peytral or its iron-plate replacement — the iron peytral remains beneath the caparison's chest panel; DO NOT add a continuous full sabaton on either boot at this level — full sabatons arrive at this level; the boots have only the existing toe-cap and ankle band as separate pieces with leather instep visible between them; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch on the spear-side hip is the only belt accessory; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT change the horse's mane shape, tail shape, ear shape, or neck arch; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, the steel-blue trim band, or the tabbed lower edge; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle or composition; DO NOT crop the rider's helm completely (top of helm should be visible); DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow — cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven.
